user.title: PowerPilz Smart Helpers
user.description: Pick the kind of helper you want to create.

menu_options.schedule: Smart Schedule — weekly plan with mode override
menu_options.timer: Smart Timer — one-shot on/off at a specific time
menu_options.curve: Smart Curve — heating curve with weekly setpoint shape

schedule.title: Smart Schedule
schedule.description:
Bundles a target device, override modes and a weekly plan in one helper.

Two modes:
• Blocks - weekly time windows (device is on during windows, off outside).
• Events - point-in-time triggers (each event fires Toggle / Pulse / a custom service).

The weekly plan is edited directly in the PowerPilz Schedule card on your dashboard - long-press the card to open the weekly editor.

data.name: Name
data.restore_auto_on_boundary: Resume Auto on next schedule boundary

data_description.target_entity: The switch, light or input_boolean this helper drives (blocks mode) or triggers at each event time (events mode).
data_description.schedule_kind: Blocks: classic time-window plan with on/off boundaries. Events: weekly point-in-time triggers, each fires the chosen action.
data_description.event_action: What each event does. Toggle calls homeassistant.toggle. Pulse turns on, waits, turns off. Custom calls a service of your choice.
data_description.pulse_duration_seconds: Seconds the target stays on during a pulse (1-900). A fixed 10s cool-down after each pulse swallows overlapping triggers.
data_description.event_service: Service to call in events+custom mode, e.g. scene.turn_on or notify.mobile_app_x.
data_description.event_service_data: Optional service data dict. entity_id is auto-filled from the target above.
data_description.mode_off_name: Display name for the Off mode.
data_description.mode_off_icon: Icon shown while in Off mode.
data_description.mode_on_name: Display name for the On mode (blocks mode only).
data_description.mode_on_icon: Icon shown while in On mode.
data_description.mode_auto_name: Display name for the Auto mode.
data_description.mode_auto_icon: Icon shown while in Auto mode.
data_description.restore_auto_on_boundary: Blocks mode only: a manual On/Off override is automatically lifted at the next schedule state change.

timer.title: Smart Timer
timer.description:
Bundles a target device with autonomous on/off-time logic in a single switch entity.

💡 After creating this helper, set the on/off times via the PowerPilz Timer card on your dashboard. The Smart Timer drives the target device autonomously — no bridging automations needed.

The target can be a regular switch/light/input_boolean, OR a select/input_select (including a PowerPilz Smart Schedule). For select targets, you'll be asked next which option to apply at each boundary.

data_description.target_entity: The switch, light, input_boolean, select or input_select this Smart Timer controls.
data_description.timer_direction: 'Both' turns the device on at the start time and off at the end time. 'On only' turns on at the start time and deactivates (no off event). 'Off only' turns off at the end time (no on event).
data_description.state_inactive_name: Label shown by the Lovelace card when the timer isn't running.
data_description.state_inactive_icon: Icon shown while the timer is idle.
data_description.state_active_name: Label shown while the timer is running.
data_description.state_active_icon: Icon shown while the timer is running.

timer_options.title: Timer select-target options
timer_options.description:
The target `{target}` is a multi-state entity. Pick which option the timer sets at each boundary.

Available options: {options}

data_description.on_option: The option to apply at the start time.
data_description.off_option: The option to apply at the end time.

curve.title: Smart Curve
curve.description:
Drives one or more heating targets with a weekly value curve. Edit the curve in the PowerPilz Heating Curve card on your dashboard — long-press the card to open the editor.

In Auto mode the helper samples the curve at the configured cadence and writes the result to every target (climate → set_temperature, number → set_value). On mode forces a fixed boost value; Off turns climate targets off.

data_description.target_entities: Climate or number entities the curve writes to. The same value is applied to every target.
data_description.update_interval_minutes: How often the curve is sampled and the targets are updated. Default 15 minutes.
data_description.value_min: Lower bound of the editor Y-axis and the value clamp.
data_description.value_max: Upper bound of the editor Y-axis and the value clamp.
data_description.mode_on_value: Constant value written while the helper is in On mode.
data_description.same_for_all_days: When enabled the editor only shows one day; the curve is mirrored across all weekdays.

error.duplicate_mode_names: The three mode names must be different.
error.targets_required: Pick at least one heating target.
error.invalid_range: Maximum must be greater than minimum.
error.invalid_event_service_data: Custom service data must be a dict.

abort.already_configured: This configuration is already set up.

set_timer.name: Set timer on/off times
set_timer.description: Update the on and/or off datetime of a PowerPilz Smart Timer.

on.name: On time
on.description: ISO 8601 datetime (e.g. 2026-04-19T18:30:00). Omit to leave unchanged.

off.name: Off time
off.description: ISO 8601 datetime. Omit to leave unchanged.

set_schedule_blocks.name: Set Smart Schedule weekly blocks
set_schedule_blocks.description: Replace the weekly schedule blocks of a Smart Schedule helper.

blocks.name: Weekly blocks
blocks.description: Per-weekday list of time blocks. Each entry has `from` and `to` times (HH:MM:SS) plus an optional `data` payload.

set_curve_points.name: Set Smart Curve weekly points
set_curve_points.description: Replace the weekly curve points of a Smart Curve helper.

points.name: Weekly points
points.description: Per-weekday list of curve points. Each entry has a `time` (HH:MM:SS) and a numeric `value`.

set_schedule_events.name: Set Smart Schedule weekly events
set_schedule_events.description: Replace the weekly point-in-time events of a Smart Schedule helper that is in events mode.

events.name: Weekly events
events.description: Per-weekday list of events. Each entry has a `time` (HH:MM:SS) and an optional `data` payload.

trigger_event_now.name: Trigger Smart Schedule event now
trigger_event_now.description: Manually fire the configured event action on a Smart Schedule helper in events mode. Works in Auto and Off modes; suppressed during pulse cool-down.
